source.title: Urinary incontinence and pelvic organ prolapse in women: management
source.population: Women aged 18 and over with urinary incontinence or overactive-bladder symptoms, with UTI, voiding, referral, and treatment qualifiers.
source.setting: Initial assessment, urine testing, bladder diaries, conservative management, and specialist referral.
source.exclusions: Partial for a sex-unspecified frequency or urgency workflow. Exact only for selected female urinary-incontinence or overactive-bladder assessment and clinician-documented conservative-management fields; medicines, procedures, fixed follow-up, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports urine dipstick, qualified culture, post-void residual, and bladder-diary documentation in adult women.

source.title: Lower urinary tract symptoms in men: management
source.population: Men aged 18 years and over with lower urinary tract symptoms, with initial, specialist, complication, and retention qualifiers.
source.setting: Initial and specialist assessment, conservative and medicine management, referral, and urinary-retention care.
source.exclusions: Exact only for adult male LUTS and urinary-retention documentation. The unrestricted legacy workflows do not encode sex or every complication qualifier; treatment, medicine choices, catheter care, surgery, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.

source.title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria
source.population: People presenting with dysuria in telehealth, with age, sex, pregnancy, comorbidity, recurrence, and complicated-infection qualifiers.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: UAE-direct for selected dysuria history, red-flag prompts, urinalysis, culture, and qualified renal-testing documentation. Physical examination, diagnosis, medicine, treatment, referral urgency, and non-telehealth pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

source.title: Lower urinary tract symptoms in men: management

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.

additions.rationale: Supports selected urinary symptom and context history.

additions.final_wording: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.

source.title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria
source.population: People presenting with dysuria in telehealth, with age, sex, pregnancy, comorbidity, recurrence, and complicated-infection qualifiers.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: UAE-direct for selected dysuria history, red-flag prompts, urinalysis, culture, and qualified renal-testing documentation. Physical examination, diagnosis, medicine, treatment, referral urgency, and non-telehealth pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports urinalysis, recommendation-qualified urine culture, and renal-function or electrolyte review in qualified pyelonephritis, nausea, or vomiting contexts.

additions.rationale: Supports clinician-ordered urinalysis and qualified culture documentation.

additions.final_wording: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.

source.title: Lower urinary tract symptoms in men: management
source.population: Men aged 18 years and over with lower urinary tract symptoms, with initial, specialist, complication, and retention qualifiers.
source.setting: Initial and specialist assessment, conservative and medicine management, referral, and urinary-retention care.
source.exclusions: Exact only for adult male LUTS and urinary-retention documentation. The unrestricted legacy workflows do not encode sex or every complication qualifier; treatment, medicine choices, catheter care, surgery, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.

additions.rationale: Supports broad LUTS identity and selected male assessment fields.

additions.final_wording: Supports acute or chronic urinary-retention context, palpable or percussable bladder, residual volume, creatinine, qualified imaging, and clinician-led follow-up or referral; catheter and medicine instructions are not mapped.

source.title: Lower urinary tract symptoms in men: management
source.population: Men aged 18 years and over with lower urinary tract symptoms, with initial, specialist, complication, and retention qualifiers.
source.setting: Initial and specialist assessment, conservative and medicine management, referral, and urinary-retention care.
source.exclusions: Exact only for adult male LUTS and urinary-retention documentation. The unrestricted legacy workflows do not encode sex or every complication qualifier; treatment, medicine choices, catheter care, surgery, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports acute or chronic urinary-retention context, palpable or percussable bladder, residual volume, creatinine, qualified imaging, and clinician-led follow-up or referral; catheter and medicine instructions are not mapped.

additions.rationale: Supports retention and bladder-distension context for adult men only.

additions.final_wording: Supports urine dipstick, qualified culture, post-void residual, and bladder-diary documentation in adult women.

source.title: Urinary incontinence and pelvic organ prolapse in women: management
source.population: Women aged 18 and over with urinary incontinence or overactive-bladder symptoms, with UTI, voiding, referral, and treatment qualifiers.
source.setting: Initial assessment, urine testing, bladder diaries, conservative management, and specialist referral.
source.exclusions: Partial for a sex-unspecified frequency or urgency workflow. Exact only for selected female urinary-incontinence or overactive-bladder assessment and clinician-documented conservative-management fields; medicines, procedures, fixed follow-up, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Urine testing, residual urine, and bladder diaries

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports urine dipstick, qualified culture, post-void residual, and bladder-diary documentation in adult women.

additions.rationale: Supports urine testing and storage-symptom assessment in adult women only.
